871.reason: [When a semicolon is missing, the parser should not consume any token but the current implementation returns the current (non-SEMICN) token as if it were consumed, causing subsequent parsing to be misaligned. Additionally, the error line number logic may be incorrect because it uses currTokenPos-1 which might not correspond to the actual last valid token of the statement.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a right parenthesis is missing, returning currToken without advancing the token pointer can cause the parser to get stuck on the same token in subsequent parsing steps, leading to infinite loops or cascading errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Similar to Judge_Error_i: when ')' is missing, returning currToken without advancing leads to potential infinite reprocessing of the same token during parsing, breaking error recovery.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [When a semicolon is missing, the parser should not consume any token but the current implementation returns the current (non-SEMICN) token as if it were consumed, causing subsequent parsing to be misaligned. Additionally, the error line number logic may be incorrect because it uses currTokenPos-1 which might not correspond to the actual last valid token of the statement.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Similar to error 'i', when a right parenthesis is missing, returning the current token without advancing can cause the parser to get stuck on the same token in subsequent parsing steps.]

888.reason: [When a right square bracket is missing, the parser should recover by assuming the bracket exists and proceed, but returning currToken without advancing prevents proper error recovery and may cause repeated processing of the same token.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a right square bracket is missing, the parser should recover by assuming the bracket exists and proceed, but returning currToken without advancing prevents proper error recovery and may cause repeated processing of the same token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: missing ']' error handling returns the current token without advancing, which can cause the parser to get stuck on the same token repeatedly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: for missing right square bracket, the parser must handle recovery properly by either skipping to a synchronization token or advancing the token pointer to avoid infinite loops.]

864.reason: [The error line number should correspond to the line of the last non-terminal before the expected semicolon, but using currTokenPos-1 may point to an incorrect token when there are missing tokens or complex expressions.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the line of the last non-terminal before the expected semicolon, but using currTokenPos-1 may point to an incorrect token when there are missing tokens or complex expressions.]

881.reason: [The error line number should correspond to the line where the last preceding non-terminal symbol appears before the right square bracket, but using currTokenPos-1 may reference an unrelated token when parsing complex array expressions.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the line where the last preceding non-terminal symbol appears before the right square bracket, but using currTokenPos-1 may reference an unrelated token when parsing complex array expressions.]
